turns.content: okay so i need to confess something and i need you to not judge me because i am judging myself enough already
No judgment here. What's going on?

i think i like country music man like actually genuinely enjoy it and i dont know how this happened to me i am a haitian man from miami who listens to kompa and hip hop and podcasts and somehow i am driving through tennessee singing along to chris stapleton at the top of my lungs
Ha! Chris Stapleton will do that to you -- the man has one of the best voices in any genre. How did this even start? Was it a gradual thing or did one song just hit you?

it was gradual like i was scanning through radio stations somewhere in kentucky a few weeks ago because my phone died and i couldnt play my podcasts and this song came on and i was like okay this is actually good and then another one came on and that was good too and before i knew it i listened to a whole hour of country radio without changing the station and i was like jerome what is happening to you
Kentucky is pretty much ground zero for that kind of ambush. Country radio out there is on another level. Do you remember what song first hooked you?

i think it was something by zach bryan like this raw kind of voice singing about working and being tired and missing people and i was like wait this is basically my life right now except he is in oklahoma and i am in a peterbilt on i-75 but the feelings are the same you know and then chris stapleton came on with tennessee whiskey and i literally pulled over to shazam it because i needed to know who this man was

Zach Bryan and Stapleton are both great entry points -- they're more roots and soul than the pop-country stuff. That rawness you're describing is what makes them connect. They're singing about real stuff -- work, loneliness, roads, missing people. Makes total sense that would resonate when you're out there driving long hauls.

right and thats the thing like i always thought country music was just about trucks and beer and i was wrong well i mean some of it is about trucks and beer but the good stuff is about loneliness and work and being far from home and loving someone you cant be with and thats literally every day of my life on the road so maybe country music was made for truck drivers or truck drivers were made for country music i dont know which came first

That's actually a pretty deep observation -- there's a real historical connection between country music and trucking culture. Songs about the road, being away from family, long nights alone. It's practically the same DNA. Have you branched out beyond Stapleton and Zach Bryan or are you still in the discovery phase?

oh im deep in it now man i made a whole playlist on spotify called road therapy and i got stapleton and zach bryan and then i found tyler childers who is insane like his voice does something to me and then someone in the comments said try sturgill simpson and i did and now im listening to metamodern sounds in country music while hauling loads through appalachia and it feels like the mountains are singing to me which sounds crazy but the music and the scenery just go together you know

Tyler Childers and Sturgill Simpson through the Appalachian mountains -- that's honestly the perfect pairing. You've got great taste too, you skipped right past the mainstream stuff and went straight to the artists that country fans argue are the real deal. Have you told anyone else about this new discovery?

man no way my cousin marc in atlanta would roast me for the rest of my life if he knew and my mom would just be confused like jerome we are haitian what are you doing and honestly when i facetime naomi last week she asked me what i was listening to and i said podcasts because i was too embarrassed to tell my nine year old daughter that her dad has become a country music fan sa k pase thats why i said dont judge me at the beginning

Your secret is safe with me. But honestly, good music is good music -- it doesn't care where you're from. And there's something great about finding a whole new genre in your mid-thirties that speaks to your actual life experience. You might be surprised too -- Naomi might love some of it.

maybe one day i will play her some tyler childers and see what she thinks she would probably be more open minded about it than marc would be anyway alright i gotta get back on the road i got about 4 more hours tonight and i think its going to be a sturgill simpson kind of night driving through west virginia in the dark with the windows cracked and the music on bondye bon life is full of surprises man a haitian truck driver singing country music in the mountains who would have thought

Sturgill through West Virginia at night sounds like the perfect drive. Enjoy it and don't feel guilty about it -- you found something that makes the long miles better and that's what matters. Safe travels out there.
